frameworks.name: deep neural networks
frameworks.one_line: End-to-end pattern recognition models trained on object/game tasks

frameworks.name: causal models of the world
frameworks.one_line: Structured generative models supporting explanation and understanding

frameworks.name: intuitive theories of physics and psychology
frameworks.one_line: Core-knowledge priors that ground and enrich learning

frameworks.name: compositionality and learning-to-learn
frameworks.one_line: Reusable structured primitives enabling rapid generalization to new tasks

core_question: What ingredients beyond current deep-network engineering are required for machines to learn and think like humans?
key_assumptions: Human-like intelligence requires more than pattern recognition
Cognitive science offers normative constraints on machine intelligence
Structured models and statistical learning are complementary, not opposed
key_findings: Deep nets match or beat humans on some object recognition, video and board game tasks
These systems still diverge from human intelligence in crucial ways
interpretations: Human-like machines must build causal world models, ground learning in intuitive physics/psychology, and exploit compositionality plus learning-to-learn
Combining neural-network strengths with structured cognitive models is the promising route forward
